mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and equity sell-offs that manifest as negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit availability restricts aggregate demand growth, forcing downward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are not significantly lower when both proxies decline simultaneously versus when only one declines.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production outpaces labor force growth, capacity constraints drive input cost inflation, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with high capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO growth decelerates below 1.0% month-over-month, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital crosses a critical threshold that forces leveraged equity positions to deleverage, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when the policy rate breaches the 5.0% threshold, or forward returns show no statistical difference between pre- and post-threshold periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real GDP signals robust aggregate demand, supporting earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns are negative or flat during quarters where real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or if the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market inflection point, accelerating recession fears and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict downward equity moves when both series deteriorate, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary growth phase, moderating consumer price inflation reduces the probability of aggressive central bank tightening, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 2.0% year-over-year, or the signal shows no predictive edge during historical disinflationary periods.
